trigger: LLM added an 'error_type' label to an existing Counter to improve granularity. All existing call sites were not updated.
symptom: TypeError: can not use inc() on LabeledCounter — call .labels('...').inc() instead
bad_code:
# Before (plain Counter, 12 call sites using .inc()):
errors_total = Counter('errors_total', 'Total errors')

# LLM changed to labeled counter without updating call sites:
errors_total = Counter('errors_total', 'Total errors', ['error_type'])

# All existing 12 call sites now fail:
errors_total.inc()   # TypeError
good_code:
# Step 1: grep ALL call sites before changing the Counter definition
# grep -rn 'errors_total' --include='*.py' .

# Step 2: Update all call sites atomically:
errors_total.labels(error_type='connection').inc()
errors_total.labels(error_type='timeout').inc()
# etc — update ALL 12 sites in the same commit
detection_command: grep -rn 'errors_total\.inc()' --include='*.py' . | wc -l  # count call sites to update
notes: CP2 (cascade propagation) co-occurs: every existing .inc() caller is a secondary impact site. Run the Interface Impact Scan from CP2 before adding any labels.
